item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, the dialogue between the space god thing and the manchild on the Icarus felt really stilted. I do like the idea overall, but having weird space God apparently torment this guy for no explained reason doesnt make sense to me, I dont see its motives for doing any of what it did.

Novote for now, honestly if you shortened the logs it would be an upvote because short but sweet is much better than the same amount of sweet stretched out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This hinges on the logs being good, and they’re not. I understand wanting to make a character easier to empathize with and feeling constrained by cold professionalism but this goes way too far in the other direction.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
gee0765,

I will try to get to as many of your minor critiques as I can, but that may take a little while. However, as you said, your main problem seems to be with the core of the article itself. Due to the fact that improving this to your standards would take a complete rewrite of the article itself, I may not address the major issues for a while, as I have already been working on this article for some time and would like to move on to other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
VaguelyDownwards,

First, if the name of the craft is really that much of a problem for you, then I'm sorry I suppose?

As for why the logs seemed unprofessional, it's because 1: I wanted to make clear that this was not created by Foundation personnel and 2: I originally had a draft where the individual acted far more professionally, but I decided that it was far more difficult to actually get the person's emotions across if they acted so… clinical.

I'm aware this won't change your opinion, but I felt you deserved an explanation. Thank you for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it until I got to the log, and then my only thoughts were 1) why do people in fiction keep naming things "Icarus" when everyone knows what happened to him, and 2) who decided such an unprofessional idiot should go into space?

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
This has been getting a few votes either way, so I thought I'd check it out:

[+ Opinion]
– hide block

Alright, first off: The blackboxes (with the possible exception of the SCP numbers but really you could choose one) detract from the experience here. They're not necessary at all. There are a few other minor things like this too, the already pointed out Icarus spacecraft name and the break in suspension of disbelief when I'm told that the Foundation had intergalactic space travel in 19711 being two of them. The occasional animation of the computer terminals is also distracting.

My main issues don't lie with those minor details though, and I could've probably overlooked most of them. The biggest problems with this are in the attached materials. Firstly, the already mentioned unprofessional actions of the dude in there. Even before there's any high stakes, he talks like a teenager or young adult, and his emotional responses massively overuse expletives, cusses and the sort of "oh god oh god" breakdown-type thing. Due to these, I felt no emotional connection to him and it wasn't a realistic character.

The logs also go on for too long before really getting anywhere. Most of the first ones, and the first "000" log, serve no purpose except being an exposition dump and there's too much of that. I was close to skimming before the story actually went anywhere. My final issue lies in the ending: I didn't like it for a few reasons. Firstly it's overly poetic in how it's said, especially for a character who has displayed no maturity up until this exact moment. Secondly, the wish would have no actual noticeable effect to his family, which I really can't see him doing since he's displayed affection towards them. Finally, I'm not a fan of the actual execution of the wish. It comes across as one of those meanie genie wording taken literally wishes; it's pretty clear that the person didn't literally want music in space.

Overall, because of the aforementioned issues, I didn't really enjoy this that much and am downvoting as a result.

Footnotes
1. I get they're technologically advanced; however, this is just too much advancement for my headcanon.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooooh ok, that makes sense. Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everything in the quoteblocks is bunched together because the ">" symbols between lines are of dialogue don't have spaces after them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll get to that!

EDIT: Actually, after having looked at the article, I'm not sure what you mean by, "The spaces in the quoteblocks didn't copy over"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I saw a previous iteration of this! Cool to see it's up again.

It looks like the spaces in the quoteblocks didn't copy over, so you may have to fix those.

item.title: Credits
item.content:encoded:
Image Source: https://www.pexels.com/photo/sky-space-telescope-universe-41951/

I would also like to thank everyone who helped me with and critiqued this article! It wouldn't be here without you.
